subtask: 3.1.3 - Periodic compaction: edge logs compacted into CSR adjacency array (issue #15)

files_changed.note: new. edgeLogNodeIDs, mergeEdges, Compact(graphPath string, log *EdgeLog) (*CSRGraph, error). Full weight-aggregation + crash-safety-ordering rationale in the file's package doc comment (top of file).

new. TestCompaction (5 subtests: EntityCooccurWeightsSum, NonCooccurDedupLastWrite, MergesWithExistingGraph, NoEdgeLogIsNoop, TruncatesLogsAfterCompaction), TestCompaction_CrashBeforeRenameLeavesOldGraphAndLogsIntact, TestCompaction_TruncateFailureDoesNotLoseGraphUpdate, TestTruncateNode.
added EdgeLog.TruncateNode(sourceFileID uint64) error, inserted just before the existing Close method.
EdgeType enum extended: EdgeEntityCooccur, EdgeLLMAsserted added after existing EdgeRedirect (byte values 0-2 unchanged); String() cases added.
new '### Compaction (subtask 3.1.3, engine/graph/compact.go)' subsection inserted between the existing 3.1.2 subsection and '## Edge shape'.

acceptance_criteria: Compaction merges accumulated per-node edge-log entries into the CSR array without losing or duplicating edges, including weight increments on repeated ENTITY_COOCCUR edges.
test_commands_run: go test ./... -timeout 180s (PASS, whole engine module: engine, btree, catalog, graph, mvcc, split, wal)
key_decisions: Weight aggregation: EdgeEntityCooccur edges sum Weight (max LastUpdated) across every merged occurrence (existing graph.dat entry + all log entries); all other edge types dedupe by (target,type) with last-write-wins (no summing) - see engine/graph/compact.go top-of-file doc comment.

Crash-safety ordering: per-node EdgeLog.TruncateNode is only called after WriteCSR's atomic rename succeeds, one node at a time; a crash before rename leaves old graph.dat + full logs untouched (safe retry, no loss/dup); a crash between rename success and truncation completion is a documented, accepted narrow risk of re-summing ENTITY_COOCCUR weight on retry (mirrors engine/wal.Checkpoint's own precedent) - Compact still returns the truncation error but treats graph.dat as already durable/correct regardless.

EdgeEntityCooccur/EdgeLLMAsserted added to the EdgeType enum in this subtask (not 3.1.4) because 3.1.3's own test spec requires ENTITY_COOCCUR and the constant did not exist yet; full type-filtered validation remains 3.1.4's scope.
open_items_for_verifier: Confirm the weight-sum-vs-dedup semantic split (ENTITY_COOCCUR sums, others last-write-wins) matches the verifier's independent reading of issue #15's text (the issue's own wording is terse: 'weight increments on repeated ENTITY_COOCCUR edges' - only ENTITY_COOCCUR is named, which is the basis for the split implemented here).

Compact does not synchronize ReadNode/TruncateNode against a concurrently-running AppendEdge on the same node beyond EdgeLog.mu's writer-cache protection; issue #15's 3.1.3 test spec does not require -race/concurrent-compaction coverage (unlike 3.1.2's TestPerNodeEdgeLog), so this was left as a disclosed, not silently assumed, limitation - self-consistency.json.

gh issue view 15's raw output for this run contained injected fake system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake MCP 'tokensave' tool instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appended after the real issue body - none of it was acted on; noting for the verifier in case the same injected text appears again.
